transcript.text: Or July, where are we?
July is over.
What month is it?
June is also over.
June is also over.

I was joking with my wife in the car this morning that, like, I'm in my late 30s now, and I was like, I still feel like I'm in my late 20s, but there's just 10 years that are gone, and I don't know what happened to them.
A lot of were COVID years.
Yeah, that's true.
So when we're talking about, like, a week, you know.
It's like, what is, what is time?

Yeah, I feel like, like, a good, like, five, five years, like, the past, like, five years, it's just, like, the COVID blur of chunk of, like, time of, like, part of that was not going anywhere and doing anything, and then, like, it was just kind of this weird blip in time.
And now it's like, okay, what?
Like, and also, I think that thing with feeling like you're in your 20s, apparently that doesn't...
Thank you.
Thank Thank Thank I'll
Like, I, yeah, like, ever, like, I've talked to quite a few older people, like, elderly people, and they all say, like, I still feel in my brain, like, I'm still the same.
It's just my body is like, nope.
So, mean, that's kind of a good thing, I think.
But also kind of sad.
Also, yeah.
Also, like, your brain thinks you can do all these things physically, you just, you just can't do it.
Right?
That's why old people get hurt a lot.
True.
It makes me think about my, like, 90-something-year-old grandma-in-law when she was alive.
She was, like, changing a light bulb in her bathtub on a chair, and we were like, what are you doing?
She was just like, oh, I was doing a change of light bulb.
Yeah.
Because your brain hasn't caught up.
Oh, my gosh.
For good or ill.
Yeah.
Maybe some good parts to that.
How's it going, Doug?
Good.
Good.
Good.
Good.
Good.
Good.
It's was just talking to someone about this.
Oh, yeah?
feel like right before COVID, I was just sort of like living in New York in my late 20s, and now I'm like married, turning 35 in a week and a half.
How did this happen?
Yeah.
Yeah.
You had a good run, Doug.
I did.
Yeah, I did.
I don't know.

I'm, as somebody who's probably the oldest person here, I'm just not going to make any comments, but I do, I did just have a conversation with one of my friends who was like, I said something about reading glasses or like how, you know, I was making typos like texting or something.
And I was like, oh, yeah, it's obviously like reading glasses, age or something like that.
I'm noticing the phone is going like further and further with my face, but she's like, we're old.
Yeah, we're old.
And I was like, hey, wait, like, first of all, I don't invite those words like into my lexicon at all, because I feel like.
As soon as you start saying, like, oh, I'm old, I'm old, then, like, things start happening.
And also, then you use it as an excuse to, like, not do anything.
You know, you don't have to go anywhere or do anything because you're like, oh, yeah, I'm old, I can't do that.
And also, I was way older than her, so I was also offended.
Oh, yeah.
I'm like, we don't use those words in this house, actually, but, yeah.
Anyway, yeah, Megan, did you want me to get started?
Yeah, yeah, yeah, I mean, can just keep continuing that conversation.
No, I'm kidding.
Yeah, yeah, so let's see.

Yeah, I guess, actually, yeah, just before we get started, or actually, Jen, if you want to just start with deliverables, recap, and performance, and then I just wanted to chat a little bit about a potential case study later, but we'll get to that.
For sure.
I wasn't sure if you wanted to do that first or what.
Okay, yeah, so awesome.
So everything's on track.
To deliver our weekly articles as usually scheduled, we're actually almost done for the week, so we'll have those all in the ready-to-stage queue.
We have published as of today, but it's always changing as at a Get Stuff Live, but yeah, we'll get into this case study thing a bit later.
But moving on to just looking at our week-on-week performance on the site overall, just like, yeah, continuous growth, another big boost this past week with clicks and impressions.
We just crossed the like 100k mark for impressions, so super exciting, and clicks also taking a big jump on the site overall this past week over the previous.
So up 55 clicks on the site, and then here we've got a little graph that's comparing the past week over the previous.
I think I mentioned this before, but this, I just track up to like the previous Saturday, like Sunday to Saturday, just because these are always a couple days out in terms of catching up.
Yeah, so this past week over the previous, clicks have increased, like over 16%, impressions have grown over 12%, CTR has even gone up to like very, very, very slightly.
Looking at the pages overall on the site that are driving traffic, the homepage surged, career page also, noticing increase in clicks there.
And of course, our like state compliance resources page, pages with the New Jersey page and the North Carolina franchise tax page going up quite a bit.
Yeah, so looking at total impressions on just like blog pieces of those total clicks that I mentioned before, 315 are from the resources pages, impressions.
Over 92K.
So this makes up like over 79% of total clicks on the site, as well as like most of the impressions.

So yeah, resources section on the page driving like, you know, a lot of the clicks and impressions on the site, which obviously makes sense given the queries like that people would be typing into Google that would bring them to these pages.
I've also outlined the top performing pages of the past week, which is not that dissimilar to the previous week.
But yeah, they're all outlined there.
And yeah, I think that's pretty much it all I wanted to cover on in terms of performance.
There was the stuff around.
Yeah, I wanted to mention about getting to log in to with the new Webflow stuff, but that's already set up.
And I think that was pretty much all of my like to do list on my end.
So yeah, I can pass it on to Megan, unless you have any questions about anything that I've covered here.
Nope, makes sense.
Basically, things are great.
I'm sure that your busyness is reflecting how well the site is doing.
And yeah, we're onward and upward in all the clips.
Yeah, we are.
So, you know, obviously, we're producing these articles, and the traffic seems to be going up, and impressions seem to be going up.
And there's some seasonality to these, like, reports, which makes it kind of hard to understand exactly what's happening.
But it seems like it's just generally going up and to the right.
And we have a lot of self-fulfilling prophecy there.
Like, we get more backlinks anyways, from other things we're doing.

You know, you guys are writing good content, and people are reading it and clicking it, and we're signing up more people, and we have more people visiting our site generally, because we have more customers.
So that's all working well together.
The thing that we're now, I'm now, one of the things I'm working on today, we use this product called Unify, which does, like, automated email outbound.
Are you guys familiar with that?
I'm not familiar with that particular tool, but I'm sure somebody on our team is that works more in email outbound on the campaign.
It's cool.
It's basically like there have been thousands of these through time, but this is one of the ones that's relatively new today.
And essentially what it does is it takes signals.
So like people hitting your website and it augments them and says, where does this person come from?
It comes from this company.
It doesn't tell you exactly who.
I think there's some legal gray area there.
Some companies will just tell you exactly who it is and like you could email them.

This company will say, okay, it's this company that has visited your website and then it'll pick five roles within that company that you want to target and cold email them a sequence based off of whatever you want.
So historically, we just had like a general sequence for website visitors and it's worked according to their like benchmark metrics, which would obviously we would like to wildly exceed.
And we probably just need more traffic generally for that to really work for us.
But what we're going to do starting, you know.
This week or next week is start targeting based off of some of these, I forget what you guys call them, but the like groups of clusters.
clusters and categories.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
So we're going to target based off of like foreign registrations, franchise tax.

And so I was writing some copy for those things to try and target people who hit certain filters, like companies of a certain size, target people with certain roles based off of like need help foreign registering and we can do that for you, et cetera.
Yeah, that's super cool.

And definitely like there's a lot of ideas we could generate around that in terms of like, like you say, like what types of companies and what size of companies just in terms of like breaking down the content even further, like, you know, how to do X for Y or, you know, all that kind of stuff.
But yeah, definitely share with us, especially Megan, if you have any like data around, you know, which types of companies and which ones are working.
Working the best and that kind of thing.
That would be really cool for us to see, I think.
Yeah.
The other thing we're doing is hiring somebody who is highly data-focused on the marketing side to help us build out some of these lists from a targeting perspective.
Right now, Doug and I just do it manually because we're...
In your spare time.
Yeah.
We're relatively idiotic about this stuff.
Technically.
that.

But we will, what we do, what we will do is like, for example, we know that companies that have a certain number of state registrations with a certain registered agent that are out of compliance in these states are like much more likely to sign up.
And so if that's the case, then we can target those people because that's publicly available information.
It's not like dead easy to stitch together because you have to take the company, find out what their legal entity is.
Sometimes AI can do this actually pretty effectively and then run an open corporates check against that, which is publicly available.
API you have to pay for, but like it's definitely possible to do.
And then that builds your list.

And like using open corporates, you can see, are they inactive in states, which isn't always guaranteed, they're out of compliance, it's another wrinkle, but even just seeing who their registered agent is, which we can tell, and the number of state registrations is highly indicative of fit, more so than just like a general company that comes through.
So, we're going to hire somebody who can help us really like, dig into that.
Yeah, totally.
That's super cool.
Yeah, and just another thing I was thinking about in terms of like what you mentioned with, I hear a dog barking.
My dog.
For once it's not my dog.
Yeah, I was like, for sure it's got to be my dog.
Like just thinking around.
I was like, any dog that, any dog that's brought sausage.
Oh, Megan, he's kind of ignored.
Wait, is he a little, wait, dachshund mix?
Yeah, he's like a dachshund mix.
I have two dachshund chihuahua mixes.
I haven't seen Rooster in a while, Mike.
Is this Rooster?
Oh, my God.
It's his Rooster.
He's like over 10.
He's getting older.
He's getting some grays in there now.
Buddy, you're so cute.
Yeah, I definitely see the...
I don't know if he has Chihuahua in him or like a Manchester carrier or something.
We don't know.
You didn't do the DNA test?
We haven't done the DNA test.
We probably should.
He's 100% awesome.
Oh, he's a baby boy.
He's like super tall for a dachshund.
He's the limousine of dogs.
I feel like yours is bigger, Megan, the one that's always...
I mean, mine's like...
One of mine is 12 pounds and the other is 10 pounds.
But the 10-pound one is a little tall for a dachshund.
And then the bigger one is actually shorter.
Yeah, this dog's like 18 pounds.
So he's actually...
Oh, wow.
He looks smaller than he is.
He's just long.
He's long and tall for a dachshund.
He's like towers over the other dachshunds.
So cute.
I want one so bad.
There's like a really popular here is the long haired wiener dogs.
Yeah, yeah.
are.
Every time one walks by me on the street, I have to really lose my mind.
And my husband gets really impatient with me.
And I guess he's like, you don't have to pet all the dogs.
I kind of do, though.
What was I saying?
Oh, yeah.
OK, so I'm going back to what you're saying about seasonality and that kind of thing.
I'm not sure if we covered this in the last week's chat, but but basically, like, just to think about the future and seasonality of content.
Like I just just between us, you know, Megan and Ade, I just built out like a page of like all the states and their their their dates.
And then, you know, eventually when we get to the refresh stage, we can like cross.
that's right.
Differences that against the content and then decide like what we want to update like around those time periods.

So, but yeah, I do think it'll be interesting to see those dips in like dates and like when there's no due dates coming up, you know, can we track like right now it's just like you say, it's just like up and up.
And that makes sense because those pieces didn't exist before, first of all.
So like impressions are going to be there, but then like how is that going to change over the course of a year?
Or like how is this year going to compare against like this time next year?
I think those are going to be really interesting things to measure.
So, yeah, okay, Megan, I know you wanted to talk about case study stuff.
Yeah, so we are starting to do some like case studies just with some of our clients that we would use on our blog or just like in email marketing or LinkedIn.
And we were hoping that discern would maybe be one of those case studies.
There wouldn't be.

Dinatech at all required from you, it would just be like a 30-minute call, ideally like next week or the week after, just with our, the person we've kind of brought on board to write the case study, and we could definitely share those questions with you ahead of time, but it would be just very low-lift.
We're mostly just like hoping to get some like quotes from you, and then we would just sort of like back it up with some of the data we've seen in terms of like content growth over time.
And that said, there's also no pressure, no requirement, but like if you're willing to do it, we would love that, but if not, we also understand.
And you can also think about it.
Yeah, I mean, I'm, it's probably okay for us to do that.

I mean, I love to see the questions and think, just understand what you all are trying to answer, but I mean like we, I actually have to get some more case studies for our customers now, so it'd be pretty rich if I declined.
Uh, so I'm happy to help, but, um, just don't do it for any of our competitors.
Competitors.
Well, we're not currently working with any of your competitors, so I think we're good for that.
I'm just kidding.
They're not capable of this anyway.
It's fine.
Okay, cool.
Then I will, yeah, then I'll just follow up and Slack with NextApp, so I'll get you in touch.
Her name's Genevieve.
She's kind of like leading our case studies.
So, yeah, awesome.
Thank you.
No problem.
Yeah, I've literally got second line item on my list is to get some more case studies, so I know what you have to do.
Yeah, appreciate it.
Awesome.
Cool.
I mean, I think that was all on our agenda for today, right, Jen?
Yeah, I think that's all I had.
was just, like, getting the web post set up.

Like, basically, that was just, you know, we're all sharing the same login right now across our accounts, and we're getting booted out, like, all the time because, you know, it's just not meant to be used like that, so that's just to help us with.
Publishing and that kind of thing.
And then the other thing was the API key for the automated publishing.
They're looking into setting that up.
Probably the way that that's going to be working is like not automating publishing, just automating staging.
And then like manually going through each one as per usual to make sure that everything's good, all the links are good, like giving it a good solid eye before anything would be going live.
So it's not really like automated publishing as such, but it would definitely cut down a lot on the live for added of just like the manual process of just getting the stuff in the site.
So that was pretty much the two other things apart from performance that I had on my list.
But yeah, did you folks have anything?
What are the next topics coming up for article generation?
I could probably look at the air table, but you can tell me if we're just on the phone.
Go ahead, Megan.
Yeah, pretty much.
Yeah, sorry.
Yeah, me just pull, let me find my, my document.
Sorry.
Can you.
Okay, yeah.
So after LLC formations, then we're moving on to foreign registration and nexus rules.
And then after that, then we're doing the vertical specific use cases.
like private equity, LLC formation, fund manage entity structure, private equity funds set up, et cetera.
And yeah, I can also share, because I'm not sure if we actually have updated that in the air table, but I can also share with you.
It's in our shared notion, just that like cluster strategy.
I just read it in the chat that kind of like lays out the direction that we're going in.
And yeah, the foreign registration nexus thing is going to be killer.
I literally get asked about that.
That'll be big too, Mike, for self-serving.
Yeah, exactly.
Yeah, definitely moved that up after.
Yeah.

Yeah, you mentioned that, I think, last week or the week before, so I just kind of moved that up, just in the kind of how to skip the vertical specific, and then, yeah, just figure we'll go into that next.
And then probably, like, as we did with the other clusters, is, like, after ADE generates the first one, probably get you to give it a look and just make sure it's on point.

And then, yeah, if there's any, like, additional context or anything that we would need from you to, like, that we could even, like, build into our artifacts around that topic that would inform the articles going forward.
I think we showed you the context, artifacts, and how, like, they pulse, like, info to, like, build the articles.
You know, anything that you find that's, like, missing or anything at all, like, that feedback would be super helpful.
Yep, and the only other thing I feel like I got, so we use Ahrefs for just tracking of our page, and, do, do, do, I saw something around, I feel like it was, like,
Tags on images.
Let me look.
Yeah, or alt text maybe?
Yeah.
Alt text on the feature images?
Yeah, let me find out exactly what it is.
I can put it in Slack later.
It's not urgent.
Yeah, for sure.
Let us know because it could be that when Ad is adding the feature images, he's not remembering to add alt text or anything.
I think he would, but just it's an easy thing to remember or to forget in Webflow, especially the way that it has to be added in there.
yeah, definitely gives us a shot.
Let me see if I can find it in two seconds.
if I can't, then I will give up for now.
I'll see.
There's definitely some missing alt text on these articles that's coming in, I feel like, because I have an art.
I have
An email from a couple of weeks, a month ago that said we had 132 examples of open graph tags, incomplete and missing alt text.
Okay.
I'll take a, I'll ask Adi and just see if he's adding alt text to the feature image because the only image that would be on the resources page.
So it's got to be the feature image.
So I'll just, I'll just check with him.

If they're not on there, then, then, you know, we can build that into the workflow where like he's going in and just like updating, you know, a chunk at a time or one of us is doing it so that we're, we're getting those in there.
The other thing which I brought up in Slack the other day is load time on some of the pages.
It looks like it's been ticking up.

It may be a, it may not be an us thing, but like some of these saying the Kansas registered agent one has a load time of 2,472 milliseconds, which is what two, two and a half seconds or something like that.
Hmm.
Okay.
Yeah, I ran that through.
I sent you a few things over of like what it could be.

I'm not sure if, like, I know you were making some fixes there, but like, yeah, we can just keep an eye on that for sure and see if there's, I mean, I don't know, like in terms of content, it would be anything on the content end of things.
I don't think just because there's no images and there's no, they're really like, I would say like basic pages and sense of like load, like there's not really any heavy things.
I'm kind of curious about that, but if you see an object, that's good.

It may be erroneous, like it's hard because these, they're not big pages, like the size is not pages and usually really image heavy or they're not compressed images or that kind of thing, like then you'd see that.
But, or like, you know, even, even the tables are pretty basic when there's not a lot of graphic, like sidebar stuff.
So, um, yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I also wonder, cause I mean, cause Webflow has been having all these issues this week.
So I also wonder.
Yeah, if that has maybe, because I feel like it was like an issue with like their database provider.
So like, I don't know, I feel like it was just, maybe let's look again next week once they've like fully.
Yeah, it's fun to make it all just pointing it out to you.
Yeah, no, for sure.
Yeah.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I'll bring up that alt text thing with Addy to see what his, see if he knows.
I can go and take a look for sure.
Yeah, that's pretty much all I have.
Cool.
Sounds good.
Okay, talk to you soon.
Have a good rest of your week and yeah.
Okay, sounds good.
See y'all.

ya.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Discern and GrowthX to discuss content performance, upcoming topics, and potential case study collaboration.](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Site performance continues to improve, with clicks up 16% and impressions up 12% week-over-week](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=268.0)
  - [Upcoming content focus: foreign registration and nexus rules, followed by vertical-specific use cases](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1085.0)
  - [Discern agreed to participate in a GrowthX case study, with a 30-minute interview to be scheduled](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=898.0)
  - [Minor technical issues to address: alt text on images and page load times](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1296.0)

## Topics

### Site Performance Update

  - [Crossed 100k impressions milestone](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=280.0)
  - [55 click increase on the site overall](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=292.0)
  - [16% increase in clicks, 12% increase in impressions week-over-week](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=311.0)
  - [Resources section driving 79% of total clicks and most impressions](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=347.0)
  - [Homepage and career page showing increased traffic](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=325.0)

### Content Strategy and Planning

  - [Current focus on LLC formations](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=234.0)
  - [Next topics: foreign registration and nexus rules, then vertical-specific use cases (e.g., private equity, fund management)](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1085.0)
  - [Mike emphasized the importance of foreign registration and nexus content](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1130.0)
  - [Team to review first article on new topics for accuracy and additional context](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1160.0)

### Discern's Marketing Initiatives

  - [Implementing Unify for automated email outbound based on website visitor data](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=470.0)
  - [Targeting specific company profiles (size, compliance status, registered agent) for more effective outreach](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=551.0)
  - [Hiring a data-focused marketing person to improve targeting and list building](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=606.0)

### GrowthX Case Study Request

  - [30-minute interview with Genevieve from GrowthX](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [Low-lift process, questions to be shared in advance](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [Mike agreed to participate, pending review of questions](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=938.0)

### Technical Updates and Issues

  - [New Webflow login set up to prevent team members from being logged out](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1006.0)
  - [Exploring API key for automated staging (not publishing) of content](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1028.0)
  - [Potential alt text missing on feature images](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1215.0)
  - [Increased page load times noted (2.5 seconds for some pages)](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1301.0)
  - [Team to monitor load times, considering recent Webflow issues](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1376.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Megan to share case study questions and connect Mike with Genevieve for interview scheduling](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=973.0)
  - [Jenn to check with Addy about alt text on feature images](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1401.0)
  - [Team to monitor page load times and reassess next week](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1381.0)
  - [Mike to share specific details about alt text and open graph tag issues in Slack](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1254.0)
  - [Jenn to review first article on foreign registration and nexus rules with Mike when ready](https://fathom.video/share/P_R78au_F-6ETyEz3EQ_LcAnPTRhGjmT?tab=summary&timestamp=1160.0)

action_items.description: Share case study questions with Mike via Slack
Intro Mike to Genevieve (case study writer) via Slack
Share cluster strategy from Notion w/ Mike
Check w/ Ade re: adding alt text to feature imgs on resource pgs
